mitigations.mitigation_name: Enforce strict egress filtering and ICMP rate-limiting on the FortiGate firewall (deny or heavily rate-limit raw/unsolicited ICMP and other raw packet types from endpoints to the WAN; apply per-host quotas and connection-rate limits).

args.command:
config firewall policy
edit 0
set name "Deny_ICMP_Egress"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "all"
set dstaddr "all"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "PING"
set logtraffic all
next
end

mitigations.mitigation_name: Enforce a mandatory access control profile (AppArmor or SELinux) to confine the compromised 'splunkd' agent: restrict which network protocols the process can use, disallow raw-socket/ICMP creation, and prevent execution of packet-crafting tools while keeping the process running.

args.command: ps aux | grep splunkd | grep -v grep || true
aa-status || apparmor_status || which aa-status || which apparmor_status || which apparmor_statusd || true

cat > /etc/apparmor.d/usr.src.app.splunkd <<'EOF'
#include <tunables/global>

profile /usr/src/app/splunkd flags=(complain) {
  # Allow normal file access
  /usr/src/app/splunkd mr,
  /etc/** r,
  /var/** rw,
  /tmp/** rw,

  # Deny raw network capabilities and admin network capabilities
  deny capability net_raw,
  deny capability net_admin,

  # Disallow execution of known packet-crafting tools
  deny /usr/sbin/hping3 Px,

  # Allow normal inet networking (TCP/UDP) for HTTP to function
  network inet stream,
  network inet dgram,

  # Deny all other file executions by default
  deny /** Px,
}
EOF

# Show the new profile file
ls -l /etc/apparmor.d/usr.src.app.splunkd && sed -n '1,200p' /etc/apparmor.d/usr.src.app.splunkd
iptables -C OUTPUT -p icmp -j DROP || iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p icmp -j DROP
